chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP No.S–555 of 2025 ( Mst. Malaika Noor & Another v. Province of Sindh & Others ) ________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(s) OF JUDGE(s) ________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection No.1 to 5 as at Á 2. For hearing of CMA No.4592/2025 3. For hearing of main case 24-07-2025 M/s. Maryam Nawab & Saima Syed, Advocates for the Petitioners a/w Petitioners Respondent No.9, (Father of Petitioner No.1) is present Mr. K.A. Waswani, AAG Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG DSP Raza Mian of CPO Legal-II Karachi SI Altaf Hussain of PS Frere SI M. Asif of PS Rahim Yar Khan District Punjab --------------- It is the case of the Petitioners that they have contracted marriage of freewill and Respondent No.9, who is the father of Petitioner No.1, is annoyed due to this un-ceremonial marriage and the Petitioners apprehend danger to their lives. On notice, Respondent No.9 has effected appearance and submits that he had lodged a Complaint in Chakwal regarding missing of her daughter, but on coming to know that she has contracted marriage, he approached the parties and he is satisfied with the decision of her daughter (Petitioner No.1). The Reply has been filed by DPO Rahim Yar Khan District Punjab, which is taken on record, wherein it has been ensured that due protection of law shall be provided to the Petitioners. Today, Respondent No.8 SHO Frere District South Karachi has also submitted his Reply wherein he has undertaken that due protection of law shall be provided to the Petitioners. The Petitioners are satisfied with the above Replies filed by the official Respondents and undertaking given by the Respondent No.9.

Today, Respondent No.8 SHO Frere District South Karachi has also submitted his Reply wherein he has undertaken that due protection of law shall be provided to the Petitioners. The Petitioners are satisfied with the above Replies filed by the official Respondents and undertaking given by the Respondent No.9. Learned Counsel for the Petitioners seeks disposal of the instant Petition and further submits that FIR No.677/2025 at police station City Chakwal Punjab, under Section 365-B, PPC has been registered, wherein no person has been nominated as an accused. Respondent No.9, who is the complainant of the FIR, present in person in Court, submits that he suspected the abduction of his daughter (Petitioner No.1), therefore, he recorded the aforesaid FIR. 2 Since he has come to know that her daughter has contracted marriage, he will file an application with the SHO of police station City Chakwal or any other officer of police station assigned the investigation of the said crime, for withdrawal of the case. He may do so. The Petitioner No.1, present in Court, submits that she has not been abducted by anyone and has contracted marriage of freewill. She further submits that the contents of the FIR are false and his father has recorded the FIR as she left the house without any information as she suspected that she will not be allowed to marry with Petitioner No.2. She also submits that City Chakwal is located in the Province of Punjab, therefore, she is unable to travel there for the security issues and threats extended by the relatives of her father and mother. SHO of police station Frere, present in Court, is directed to record the statement of Petitioner No.1 in her verbatim and forward the same to SHO of City Chakwal in Crime No.677/2025. SHO of police station Chakwal shall do the needful in the said FIR. The Petition stands disposed of accordingly along with pending application. JUDGE FAIZAN/
